articles.summary: Israel preparing to intercept Gaza-bound aid flotilla, local media says Israeli media reported that Israel is preparing to intercept a Gaza-bound aid flotilla as dozens of vessels approach the Gaza Strip in an attempt to challenge the Israeli blockade. Activists aboard the 54-ship Global Sumud Flotilla earlier reported spotting a military vessel near the convoy, which is expected to reach Gaza’s waters within two days. articles.title: Former IRGC commander calls US naval blockade ‘act of war’
articles.summary: Former IRGC commander calls US naval blockade ‘act of war’ Iranian official Mohsen Rezaei warned the United States to end its blockade of Iranian ports, saying Tehran’s military remained prepared for further confrontation. Speaking on Iranian state television, the former Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps commander said: “We advise the US military to end the siege before the Sea of Oman becomes your graveyard.” Rezaei described the blockade as an “act of war” and said Iran had the right to respond. “The longer they prolong the naval blockade of Iran, the greater the damage to countries around t

articles.summary: China’s growth slowed in April with investment declining and retail sales rising just 0.2%. articles.title: Israeli strike in eastern Lebanon kills Islamic Jihad leader, daughter
articles.summary: Israeli strike in eastern Lebanon kills Islamic Jihad leader, daughter The National News Agency reported early Monday that an Israeli missile strike near Baalbek in eastern Lebanon killed a leader of Palestinian Islamic Jihad and his daughter. The strike reportedly targeted an apartment housing a Palestinian family on the outskirts of the city. The attack came amid continuing Israeli operations despite a fragile ceasefire agreement announced on 17 April and extended on Friday for another 45 days through early July.

articles.summary: Samsung Electronics Co.’s shares jumped after company management entered make-or-break wage negotiations with its largest labor union aimed at averting a strike that could disrupt operations at the world’s biggest memory chipmaker.

articles.summary: Iran’s foreign ministry spokesman taunts Trump with movie clip Iranian foreign ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei shared a clip from The Apprentice, a biographical film about US President Donald Trump’s early career, as part of a broader wave of Iranian messaging criticising Washington. The scene posted by Baghaei features lawyer Roy Cohn outlining “rules for winning”, including “no matter what happens, you claim victory and never admit defeat”. Baghaei’s post on X came amid growing criticism by Iranian officials of Trump’s handling of the conflict. Baghaei has previously referenced literar

articles.title: Former US defence chief says he rejected Netanyahu’s Iran assessment
articles.summary: Former US defence chief says he rejected Netanyahu’s Iran assessment Former US defence secretary and CIA director Robert Gates recalled a 2009 conversation with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in which Netanyahu argued that Iran would quickly collapse if attacked militarily. Speaking on Face the Nation, Gates said Netanyahu told him the Iranian government would “crumble at the first attack”. Gates said he rejected the argument, telling Netanyahu he was “dead wrong” and underestimated “the resilience of the Iranians”. He also said Israeli strikes on Iraq’s Osirak reactor in 1981 and a

articles.summary: Lebanese and Kuwaiti leaders discuss regional tensions Lebanese President Joseph Aoun spoke with Kuwaiti Emir Mishal Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah about regional developments and tensions affecting their countries, according to the National News Agency. Aoun expressed “the solidarity of the Lebanese people with their Kuwaiti brothers and sisters during these critical times the region is experiencing”, the agency reported. articles.title: Ulyanov suggests Iran appoint special representative to Russia
articles.summary: Ulyanov suggests Iran appoint special representative to Russia Russian envoy Mikhail Ulyanov suggested that Iran consider appointing a special envoy to Russia similar to the arrangement Tehran recently established with China. In a post on X, Ulyanov responded to reports that Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf had been appointed as a special envoy to China. “Isn’t it equally relevant to consider something similar in relations with Russia?” Ulyanov wrote.

articles.title: Houthis reaffirm support for Hamas after commander’s killing
articles.summary: Houthis reaffirm support for Hamas after commander’s killing Yemen’s Houthis reaffirmed support for Palestinian armed groups in the Gaza Strip following the reported killing of senior Hamas commander Izz al-Din al-Haddad. In a condolence message posted on Telegram, Houthi military leader Yusuf Hassan al-Madani described al-Haddad’s death as a major loss for the Palestinian movement and the wider region. Al-Madani said the Houthis would continue what he called joint efforts with Hamas and allied factions “until victory is achieved”.

articles.title: French opposition leader attacks ‘European complicity’ in Iran conflict
articles.summary: French opposition leader attacks ‘European complicity’ in Iran conflict French opposition leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon condemned what he described as “European complicity” in the US and Israeli strikes on Iran that triggered a wider regional conflict. In a post on X, Mélenchon said: “Trump and Netanyahu’s crude threats to destroy Iran are only possible thanks to European complicity.” He added: “The world’s peace is reduced to the interests of Trump and Netanyahu’s colonialism.” The leading figure of La France Insoumise, who is expected to contest the 2027 French presidential election, also urged

articles.title: Iran discusses Strait of Hormuz with French and South Korean ministers
articles.summary: Iran discusses Strait of Hormuz with French and South Korean ministers Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi held talks with French and South Korean officials on regional developments and ongoing diplomatic efforts linked to tensions in the Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz. Iran said Araghchi and his French counterpart discussed “bilateral issues, the latest regional developments, and current diplomatic trends”. articles.title: Charity workers killed in Israeli strike on community kitchen in Gaza
articles.summary: Charity workers killed in Israeli strike on community kitchen in Gaza Israeli attacks across the Gaza Strip killed at least five Palestinians on Sunday, including three charity workers in Deir el-Balah, according to reports from Gaza. Additional deaths were reported in Khan Younis and Beit Lahiya. Al Jazeera reported that the strike in Deir el-Balah targeted a community kitchen. Hamas described the attack as “a deliberate war crime and a renewed scene of the ongoing genocide against our people in the Gaza Strip”.

articles.title: Araghchi and Fidan hold talks on regional tensions
articles.summary: Araghchi and Fidan hold talks on regional tensions Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araghchi spoke with Turkish foreign minister Hakan Fidan about the latest developments in the stalled negotiations between the United States and Iran, as well as broader regional tensions. Turkey has been working diplomatically behind the scenes to help end the conflict while also strengthening security along its border with Iran amid concerns that worsening conditions could lead to a large-scale displacement of people into the country.

articles.title: Palestinian ministry condemns Israeli property seizure plan near Al-Aqsa
articles.summary: Palestinian ministry condemns Israeli property seizure plan near Al-Aqsa The Palestinian Ministry of Foreign Affairs condemned an Israeli plan to seize up to 20 properties near the Al-Aqsa Mosque in occupied East Jerusalem, calling it “ethnic cleansing”. The properties are located in the Bab as-Silsila area adjacent to the mosque, considered Islam’s third-holiest site. The ministry said the move was part of a broader effort by Israel to gradually displace Palestinians from the area while expanding the number of Israeli residents. Israeli settlements in the occupied Palestinian territories are

articles.title: Gaza aid flotilla activist reports military vessel near convoy
articles.summary: Gaza aid flotilla activist reports military vessel near convoy A Spanish activist aboard one of dozens of vessels sailing toward the Gaza Strip said a ship believed to be military had approached the flotilla, attempting to break Israel’s blockade. Activist Pablo Quesada Martin wrote on X that a vessel appearing to be military had been spotted near the convoy, leading organisers to activate security protocols. Israel has previously intercepted and detained activists seeking to deliver humanitarian aid to Gaza. Last month, Israeli forces intercepted 22 vessels near the Greek island of Crete.

articles.title: Israel extends death penalty law to Palestinians in West Bank
articles.summary: Israel extends death penalty law to Palestinians in West Bank Israel’s Central Command chief, Major General Avi Bluth, has signed an order extending the application of Israel’s death penalty law to Palestinians in the occupied West Bank. The move follows internal discussions in which legal and security officials warned against applying civilian legislation passed by the Knesset in territory where most residents are not Israeli citizens. The law targets those accused of acts carried out “with the aim of denying the existence of the State of Israel”. In practice, its application in the West Bank

articles.title: Saudi Arabia intercepts three drones entering from Iraq
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia intercepts three drones entering from Iraq Saudi Arabia said it intercepted and destroyed three drones that entered its airspace from Iraq on Sunday morning. In a statement, the Ministry of Defence said the drones were neutralised after crossing into Saudi territory. It added that the kingdom “reserves the right to respond” and will take “operational measures” against any attempt to violate its sovereignty and security.
